title: baccharis macrantha var. pastoensis
intro: Baccharis macrantha var. pastoensis, also known as pasto baccharis, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the Asteraceae family. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and aiding in the treatment of wounds, due to its high concentration of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. Baccharis macrantha var. pastoensis can be prepared as an infusion, decoction, or tincture, and is often used in herbal medicine to promote overall well-being and alleviate various health conditions.
